fix(aks-gitops-platform): remove invalid Nunjucks expression from terraform.tfvars comment

The comment '# This file uses ${{values.*}} expressions' was being parsed
by Backstage's Nunjucks template engine as a template variable expression.
values.* is invalid Nunjucks syntax and caused the fetch:template step to fail.

Replaced the comment with plain text that describes the same intent without
using the ${{ }} template delimiter pattern.

feat(cli): target-aware native link plan + explicit Darwin contract (#968)

* feat(cli): target-aware native link plan + explicit Darwin contract (#254)

Introduce NativeLinkPlan in target.rs to make every platform-specific
linker decision derive from the target TargetOs rather than the
compile-time host #[cfg] world.

### hew-cli/src/target.rs
- New NativeLinkPlan struct: gc_flags, strip_flags, platform_libs,
  needs_darwin_sdk, needs_windows_crt_fixup, needs_dsymutil.
- New TargetSpec::native_link_plan() returning the correct plan for
  Darwin, Linux, Windows, and WASM targets.
- 10 new unit tests covering all platform plan fields independently of
  the running host.

### hew-cli/src/link.rs
- link_executable uses target.native_link_plan() for all target-driven
  flag groups; #[cfg] blocks replaced by plan field dispatch:
  - plan.gc_flags / plan.strip_flags replaces the macos/unix cfg blocks.
  - plan.needs_darwin_sdk gates find_darwin_sdk() (new wrapper).
  - plan.needs_windows_crt_fixup gates the CRT override args.
  - plan.platform_libs replaces per-platform cfg lib blocks.
  - plan.needs_dsymutil gates run_dsymutil_for_darwin() (new wrapper).
- Host-driven sections (compiler/lld selection) explicitly labeled.
- FreeBSD fallback kept under #[cfg(target_os = "freebsd")] with a
  FOLLOW-UP note pending TargetOs::FreeBsd addition.

Behaviour preserved: identical linker invocations for all currently
supported targets. Darwin cross-arch (arm64 <-> x86_64) and same-arch
native links validated by e2e tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  hew-cli/src/target.rs:619 - windows_link_plan_platform_libs

fix: correct post-migration token calculation

CRITICAL BUG FIX: Post-migration estimate was incorrectly set to 0 tokens.

Progressive-disclosure architecture doesn't eliminate skills from context:
- L1 metadata (name + description) loads at startup for all vaulted skills
- Each skill's L1 metadata costs ~100 tokens for capability discovery
- Actual skill bodies load only when explicitly called via load_skill()

Correct formula:
  projected_tokens = global_tokens + (vaultable_skill_count × 100)

Previous (wrong):
  projected_tokens = global_tokens  # Ignored L1 metadata cost entirely

Impact on accuracy:
- Tool now honestly reports when migration would increase startup tokens
  (when skills are small: avg_skill_tokens < 100)
- Enables developers to make informed trade-off decisions
- Maintains audit integrity for token-honest project analysis

Updated tests to validate correct behavior:
- L1 metadata counted in projected startup cost
- Reduction percentage can be negative when skills benefit from being left ungated

Fixes #1 (implicit): Token audit integrity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(config): Fix reports.register() failing with "Cannot call TaskOutputs.file() after task started execution" (#1618)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve reports.register() failing with TaskOutputs.file() error during execution

When using reports.register() for lazy report registration, the domain objects
are realized at iteration time. Previously, the factory function called
(outputs as TaskOutputs).file() which fails if called during task execution.

Fix: remove the outputs.file() call from the factory and instead register all
required reports' output files via a lazy provider in the init block. The
provider is evaluated at task-graph finalization (before execution), not during
the task action itself.

Also adds a functional test covering the exact scenario from issue #1539:
registering multiple reports with register() without explicit required=true.

Fixes #1539

Co-authored-by: hazendaz <975267+hazendaz@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct output-tracking for lazily-registered reports

The previous approach had two bugs caught by CI:

1. ArchUnit violation: outputs.files() without explicit cast caused Kotlin
   to infer AbstractTask.getOutputs() return type as TaskOutputsInternal
   (internal Gradle API). Fix: cast to org.gradle.api.tasks.TaskOutputs.

2. Lazy items excluded from output tracking: Kotlin's Collection.filter{}
   calls Iterable.iterator() which only returns already-realized items.
   Items registered with reports.register() stay pending/lazy and are
   invisible to iterator(), so the provider returned an empty file list.
   Fix: use reports.matching{} (Gradle's API) which realizes pending items
   when the resulting collection is iterated.

Also uses an anonymous Callable object instead of a lambda, following the
existing NamedDomainObjectFactory pattern for configuration-cache safety.

Fixes #1539

Co-authored-by: hazendaz <975267+hazendaz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

Co-authored-by: hazendaz <975267+hazendaz@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: pre-realize pending reports to prevent config-cache ConcurrentModificationException

When Gradle serializes the task state for the configuration cache, it
visits the reports container's internal pending map. If any reports were
registered lazily via reports.register(), the serializer encounters
ProviderInternal objects in that map; realizing those providers moves
entries out of the map while the serializer is still iterating it,
causing a ConcurrentModificationException.

Fix: after executing the user's configuration action inside reports{},
call reports.toList() to force-realize every pending item. This empties
the pending map before the configuration-cache serializer ever visits it,
so the ConcurrentModificationException no longer occurs.

Fixes #1539

Co-authored-by: hazendaz <975267+hazendaz@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
core: fix market-status early-close calendar semantics

The early_close_holidays check was dead code — the holiday return
fired before it could run.  The early_close_eves config used holiday
names that didn't match the holidays library output (e.g.
'Christmas Eve' vs 'Christmas Day').

Fix the control flow: determine early-close status BEFORE the holiday
return so same-day half-holidays are treated as early close, not full
closure.  Add early_close_day_after field for day-after-holiday half
days (Black Friday).  Fix HKEX and ASX configs to match against the
actual holiday name the library produces.

Includes tests for same-day, day-after, and eve rule types plus
upcoming holiday impact metadata.

commit.message:
Concurrency hardening: fix TLS leak, OnDetach crash, and 12 thread-safety issues (#24)

* Fix session saga support: null state, message lock, thread-safe SessionManager

Three fixes for session-based saga scenarios (e.g. MassTransit session sagas):

1. GetSessionState returns null instead of empty byte array when no state
   is set, matching real ASB. Previously MassTransit's JSON deserializer
   crashed on the empty bytes.

2. Session-enabled queues skip per-message lock expiry checks and sweep.
   In real ASB the session lock governs all messages in the session —
   individual message locks don't expire independently.

3. Thread-safe SessionManager creation via Interlocked.CompareExchange
   instead of racy ??= operator.

Adds conformance tests for session lock renewal, session processor with
auto-renewal, and session state round-trip with scheduling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add session diagnostics and fix premature session lock release

Add Warning-level logging to trace session saga failures:
- EmulatorContainer: log entity management link creation/reuse/fallback
- ManagementLinkEndpoint: log session lock renewal, get/set state, schedule-message
- ServiceBusLinkProcessor: log session receiver acceptance (immediate and polled)
- QueueEntity: log session queue enqueue with SessionId
- SessionManager: add GetSessionIds() diagnostic method

Fix SessionReceiverLinkEndpoint.OnLinkClosed releasing session locks
immediately, which races with the Azure SDK's auto-renewal timer and
causes ErrorRequiresRecycle cascades. Session locks now expire naturally
via LockDuration, matching real Azure Service Bus behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix #1: SessionManager.TryAcceptSession TOCTOU race condition

Add lock to TryAcceptSession to prevent multiple concurrent receivers
from grabbing the same session. The check-then-act on IsLocked + write
to LockedBy/LockedUntil was completely unsynchronized, allowing N
threads to all see IsLocked==false and all proceed to lock the session.

Stress test: 200 iterations x 20 concurrent receivers, 100% failure
rate before fix, 0% after.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix #2/#3/#10: Pump double-start and CTS field capture in link endpoints

Add lock(_pumpLock) around the check-then-act on _pumpTask in both
ReceiverLinkEndpoint.OnFlow and SessionReceiverLinkEndpoint.OnFlow.
Without this, concurrent OnFlow calls could each start a message pump,
causing multiple pumps to dequeue from the same queue simultaneously.

Also fix event handler closure capture: lambdas now close over a local
`cts` variable instead of the `_pumpCts` field, preventing old link
Closed handlers from cancelling the wrong (newer) CancellationTokenSource.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix #4/#9: Protect all SessionManager lock mutations with _acceptLock

ReleaseSession, RenewSessionLock, and GetAvailableSessionIds now hold
the _acceptLock, preventing torn reads of LockedBy/LockedUntil (12-byte
DateTimeOffset is not atomically writable) and TOCTOU races where
RenewSessionLock could extend an already-released session.

Stress test: 10 threads x 500 iterations of concurrent accept/release
cycles, 0 double-hold violations across all runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix #5: Close TOCTOU window in SweepExpiredLocks vs Complete

The sweep now writes to _sweptLockTokens BEFORE calling TryRemove on
_pending. This closes the window where a concurrent Complete() could
see the message missing from both dictionaries (already removed from
_pending but not yet in _sweptLockTokens), silently return, and then
the sweep re-enqueues — causing duplicate delivery.

If the sweep's TryRemove fails (another thread settled first) or the
lock was renewed (re-check sees valid LockedUntil), the swept marker
is cleaned up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix #6: Make BrokeredMessage DeliveryCount and LockedUntil thread-safe

DeliveryCount now uses Volatile.Read/Write with an IncrementDeliveryCount()
method backed by Interlocked.Increment — the old DeliveryCount++ was a
non-atomic read-modify-write that could lose increments under contention.

LockedUntil now stores UTC ticks as a long field with Interlocked.Read/
Exchange, eliminating torn reads of the 12-byte DateTimeOffset struct.
The property getter/setter converts transparently.

Stress tests: 20 threads x 1000 increments = 20000 exact; concurrent
read/write of LockedUntil detects zero torn values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix #7: DeadLetterQueue double allocation leaking Timer

Replace ??= with Interlocked.CompareExchange pattern. The old ??= was
not atomic: two threads could both see null, both allocate a QueueEntity
(each starting a Timer), and the loser's instance was leaked. The new
pattern disposes the losing instance immediately.

Stress test: 50 concurrent threads access DeadLetterQueue on a fresh
queue — all get the same instance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix #8: ScheduledMessageProcessor Start/Dispose lifecycle race

Add lock(_lifetimeLock) around both StartBackground and Dispose.
StartBackground now cancels/disposes any existing CTS before creating
a new one, preventing leaked background tasks when called twice.
Dispose holds the same lock to prevent racing with StartBackground.

Stress tests: 50 rapid start/dispose cycles complete without deadlock;
double-start cancels the previous task cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix #11/#12: Atomic LastActivityAt and lock DispatchRequest log read

NamespaceContext.LastActivityAt now stores UTC ticks as a long field
with Interlocked.Read/Exchange, preventing torn reads of the 12-byte
DateTimeOffset struct when Touch() is called from concurrent threads.

EmulatorContainer.DispatchRequest now reads entry.ResponseLinks.Count
inside the lock instead of outside, preventing a data race on the
non-thread-safe Dictionary during concurrent link attachment/detachment.

Completes the 12-issue concurrency hardening pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace concurrency dashboard with markdown report

Remove the auto-refreshing HTML dashboard (served its purpose during
the fix session) and add a concise markdown report documenting all
12 issues, fixes, stress test results, and recommendations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Encapsulate SessionState lock fields behind internal mutation methods

SessionState.LockedBy and LockedUntil are now private fields with
read-only public getters. All lock mutations go through internal
methods (TryLock, Unlock, RenewLock) that are only callable from
within the assembly. This makes it structurally impossible for code
outside SessionManager to corrupt lock state.

LockedUntil is stored as long ticks with Interlocked for atomic reads
(consistent with BrokeredMessage.LockedUntil from the earlier fix).
GetSessionIds now also holds _acceptLock for a consistent snapshot.

ReaderWriterLockSlim was considered and rejected: the access pattern is
write-heavy (accept/release/renew), critical sections are 2-3 assignments,
and the only pure readers are diagnostics. The Lock type is the right
choice — lower overhead, simpler, and RWLS only pays off with many
concurrent readers holding the lock for significant durations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix TLS proxy backend TcpClient leak causing port exhaustion under load

HandleTlsConnection created backend TcpClients as local variables that
were never disposed — HandleConnectionAsync's finally block only tracked
backends for non-TLS paths. Every AMQPS/HTTPS connection leaked a socket
handle + ephemeral loopback port, exhausting ports at ~500 concurrent
orders from multiple processes.

Wrap backend lifecycle in try/finally, dispose SslStream deterministically,
set TCP backlog to 512, and upgrade TLS handshake failure logging to Warning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Reduce AMQP log noise: Warning→Debug for routine session/management ops

Connection tracking, session accept/release, schedule-message, and
management link operations were logged at Warning, drowning out real
issues under load. Downgrade to Debug so Warning-level logs surface
actual problems (like the TLS leak).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add /diagnostics endpoint for thread pool and memory stats

Exposes worker/IO thread availability, pending work items, working set,
and GC collection counts — useful for diagnosing resource exhaustion
under concurrent load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add session load tests reproducing Black Friday multi-process failure

- SessionLoadTests: 200-1000 messages across 2-50 sessions (plain AMQP)
- SessionTlsLoadTests: 500-2000 messages through TLS proxy path
- MassTransitSessionLoadTests: 500-2000 messages with MassTransit sessions
- Session auto-renewal conformance test (3s lock, 10s hold)

These reproduce the port exhaustion bug that broke the OrderFlowDemo at
~500 completed orders under concurrent multi-process TLS load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rewrite run.ps1: separate terminals, pre-build, configurable log level

Launch each process in its own cmd window for clean log output. Pre-build
all projects to avoid concurrent build conflicts. Add -LogLevel parameter
(defaults to Warning).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix OnDetach crash: intercept Start-state Detach via IHandler before state machine

When a session receiver link is waiting for a session (Attach not yet
completed), the link stays in AMQPNetLite's Start state. If the client
sends Detach (timeout/disconnect), AMQPNetLite throws AmqpException
("OnDetach is not valid under state: Start") which kills the entire
AMQP connection and all its links.

Fix: AMQPNetLite calls IHandler.Handle(LinkRemoteClose) BEFORE the
state machine check. In GuidDeliveryTagHandler (already installed on
every connection via HandlerFactory), intercept LinkRemoteClose events
for links in Start state and transition them to Attached via reflection.
The subsequent state check passes and the Detach is handled gracefully.

Verified: 3167 Black Friday orders, 312 handler interventions,
0 illegal-state crashes (previously 2-4 per run).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Disable test parallelism in MassTransit tests to fix port 5672 collision

All MassTransit tests use UseDevelopmentEmulator=true which requires a
fixed port 5672. When xUnit runs test classes in parallel, multiple
fixtures try to bind the same port causing "Address already in use".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix flaky CI tests: PumpGuard timing and MassTransit startup race

- PumpGuard: Task.Delay(10) completed before all threads checked on slow
  CI, allowing a legitimate second pump start. Use a never-completing
  TaskCompletionSource instead.
- MassTransit SessionQueue_HighLoad: 3s fixed delay wasn't enough on
  slow CI. Poll bus health until Healthy before sending messages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Only bind management port 5300 when fixture uses a fixed port

When dotnet test runs all test projects in parallel, every fixture tried
to bind port 5300 for the management API. Only MassTransit tests need it
(UseDevelopmentEmulator=true hardcodes management to 5300). Other test
projects use random ports and create entities directly via the registry.

Now only fixtures with a fixed public port (MassTransit) bind 5300,
eliminating the port collision that caused 0/500 consumed on CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Increase scheduling test margin to 8 seconds (drop warm-up approach)

commit.message:
feat: add progress feedback for large collection fetching (#21)

Closes #20
Large collections (hundreds of items) produce no output while resolving
external IDs per-item via HTTP, making the tool appear hung.

### Changes

- **`plex.Client.OnProgress` callback** — called with `(current, total)`
after each item is processed in `GetCollectionItems`, where the per-item
metadata lookups are the bottleneck
- **High-level status in `resolveCollection`** — prints
finding/fetching/loaded messages to stdout so the user sees activity
immediately
- **Stderr progress line in `main()`** — `newCollectionProgressFunc`
writes `\rProcessing collection items: N/M` with in-place
carriage-return updates; newline on completion

```
Finding collection "Movies"...
Fetching items for "Movies"...
Processing collection items: 142/200   ← stderr, in-place
Loaded 200 items from "Movies"
```

The callback is nil-safe — no behavior change when unset (e.g., in tests
or the bridge package).
